growth_records.lvlwhy: the source paper (often a review/modeling reuse) states no strain
growth_records.medium: none (obligate host-associated growth; not axenically cultured)
growth_records.cite: Harrison et al. Mutation rates and the molecular epidemiology of Mycobacterium abscessus. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2023). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2307499120. [PMC10288644]
growth_records.snippet: it has a doubling time of 12 d and is propagated in 9-banded armadillos, among its many eccentric features.
growth_records.notes: This is a Commentary article; the M. leprae doubling time (12 days) is stated as established background fact (not primary data generated in this paper), with no defined culture medium since M. leprae is grown in vivo in armadillo hosts, not axenic culture. mu = ln2/(12 d x 24 h/d) = 0.693147/288 h = 0.0024 h-1. Not informative for constraining a defined-medium GEM; included per instruction to capture every reported doubling time, flagged low confidence.
